name: Commonwealth National Bank
description_short: Commonwealth National Bank is a Black-owned community bank headquartered in Mobile, Alabama, offering personal and business banking, loans, and credit cards with local service and digital convenience.
description_long:
Commonwealth National Bank was founded in coastal Alabama and operates as one of only 14 Black-owned banks in the United States. The bank was established with a mission to serve its community and make wealth accessible, operating from its main office in Mobile, Alabama. The institution is FDIC-insured and backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, providing customers with deposit protection and security.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of financial products for both personal and business customers. Personal services include checking and savings accounts, the newly launched Common Freedom Account, certificates of deposit (CDs), consumer loans, mortgage products, and credit cards with rewards and cash back options. Business customers can access business checking and savings accounts, business loans, competitive CDs, and integration with QuickBooks for accounting purposes. The bank also provides digital banking through online and mobile platforms, allowing customers to manage accounts, deposit checks, transfer funds, and locate ATMs around the clock.

Commonwealth distinguishes itself through its community-focused approach and personal service philosophy. The bank emphasizes that customers are "more than just a number" and considers the entire financial journey rather than relying solely on credit scores. As one of the few Black-owned banks in the country, it has a proven track record of serving its community. The bank offers access to a surcharge-free ATM network including Commonwealth branches and partner institutions (Bank of America, PNC, Wells Fargo, Regions, and Trustmark) nationwide. The institution also provides free financial wellness tools through its "Our Money Matters" program powered by Enrich, a personal finance platform designed to help customers reach financial goals.

Commonwealth National Bank is best suited for individuals and businesses seeking banking services with a community-focused approach and local expertise in the Gulf Coast region. The bank's emphasis on comprehensive financial consideration beyond credit scores may appeal to customers with non-traditional credit histories. However, potential customers should note that the bank's physical presence appears limited to Mobile, Alabama locations, which may restrict accessibility for those outside the Gulf Coast area despite digital banking options. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Certificates of Deposit (CDs) for personal and business customers
Consumer personal loans with competitive rates and fast approvals
Mortgage loans (conventional, FHA, VA, USDA, and jumbo)
Credit cards with rewards points and cash back options
Secured credit cards for credit rebuilding
Online and mobile banking with check deposit, fund transfers, and ATM locating
Financial wellness education through Our Money Matters program
Debit and ATM card replacement at branches
pros: One of only 14 Black-owned banks in the United States with established community track record
FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of the U.S. Government
Surcharge-free ATM access nationwide through partner network (Bank of America, PNC, Wells Fargo, Regions, Trustmark)
Free personal finance platform (Our Money Matters powered by Enrich) to help customers reach financial goals
Newly launched Common Freedom Account with no monthly fees and online opening capability
Credit cards with both rewards points and cash back options, including secured cards for credit rebuilding
Competitive mortgage products including FHA/VA/USDA and jumbo loans with fast turnaround times
cons: Physical banking locations limited to Mobile, Alabama area despite digital banking options
Website content is incomplete and cuts off mid-sentence in several sections, suggesting incomplete information
Limited details provided about specific loan terms, APR rates, fees, and eligibility requirements
No information on minimum deposit amounts, account minimums, or CD rates
Mobile app appears to be newly launched with no user reviews or detailed feature information available
best_for: Individuals and businesses in the Gulf Coast/Mobile, Alabama area seeking community-focused banking
Customers with non-traditional credit histories who value holistic financial assessment beyond credit scores
Business owners needing local expertise and QuickBooks integration for accounting
Customers seeking to build or rebuild credit through secured credit cards and community-focused lending
diagnosis: Commonwealth National Bank is best for individuals and businesses in the Mobile, Alabama area or Gulf Coast region seeking community-focused banking with personal service and a financial institution committed to serving customers beyond credit scores. The main caveat is that despite offering digital banking, the bank's physical presence and primary service area appear concentrated in Mobile, Alabama, which may limit accessibility for customers outside this geographic region.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
